reasoning: The FRED data shows that AA corporate bond spreads recently spiked from 0.51 on Mar 5 to 0.60 on Mar 13, 2026, reaching their highest level since early 2025. Given that this rapid increase leaves the spread significantly above its 2026 baseline trend, mean reversion is highly probable over a short 1-week horizon. The statistical model estimates a baseline probability of a further increase at roughly 32%. Furthermore, the March 2026 FOMC meeting is set to conclude on March 18; standard market behavior typically involves credit spreads tightening or plateauing once major macroeconomic event risk is resolved. Coupled with strong fundamental demand for investment-grade credit (which recently drove spreads to multi-decade lows in January 2026), these factors suggest the spread is much likelier to stabilize or drop than to continue climbing above 0.60. Adjusting the 32% model estimate slightly downward for post-FOMC spread compression, I estimate a 28% chance of an increase.
